34.reason: // The assignment 'p = getchar() != '\n'' is incorrect due to operator precedence; it assigns the result of the comparison (0 or 1) to p, not the character. Should be: while((p = getchar()) != '\n')

42.reason: // Incorrect indexing: l and y arrays store alternating coefficient and exponent, but this assumes even indices are exponents and odd are coefficients without validating input length parity. Also, if m or n is odd, 2*c may exceed bounds.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.1111111111111111 , reason:  // Incorrect indexing: l and y arrays store alternating coefficient and exponent, but this assumes even indices are exponents and odd are coefficients without validating input length parity. Also, if m or n is odd, 2*c may exceed bounds.

43.reason: // Same indexing issue: assumes valid pairs exist up to (m+1)/2 and (n+1)/2, which may not hold if input has odd number of integers.

47.reason: // Passing &a[1] and &b[1] implies 1-based indexing, but C arrays are 0-based; this leads to undefined behavior when accessing a[n] inside the function since a[0] is skipped and a[e] may go out of bounds.
